name: Harvest Cornish Hens
recipeIngredient: 4 1 3/4 lb. fresh or frozen Cornish hens
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblets and necks from Cornish hens; refrigerate to use in soup another day.
2. Rinse hens with running cold water; pat dry with paper towels.
3. Sprinkle inside body cavity with salt and pepper.
4. Fold neck skin to the back; lift wings toward the neck, then fold them under the back of the hen so they stay in place.
5. Tie legs and tail of each hen together with string.
6. Place hens, breast-side up, on a rack in a roasting pan.
7. In a cup, mix olive or salad oil, dried parsley flakes, dried thyme leaves, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper; brush hens with the oil mixture.
8. Roast hens in a 350 degree oven about 1 1/4 hours, brushing occasionally with drippings from the pan.
9. Hens are done when legs can be moved up and down easily or when a fork inserted between the leg and body cavity and juices that escape are not pink.
10. Toward the end of roasting hens, in an 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook carrots, Brussels sprouts, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in hot margarine until golden.
11. Add 1/2 cup water; over high heat, bring to boiling. Reduce heat to medium-low; cover and cook for 5 to 10 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp, stirring occasionally; keep warm.
12. When hens are done, discard strings. Place hens on a warm large platter; keep warm.
13. For gravy, remove the rack from the roasting pan; pour drippings into a 1-cup measure. Let drippings stand a few seconds until fat separates from meat juice. Skim 2 tablespoons fat from drippings into a 3-quart saucepan; skim and discard any remaining fat.
14. In the saucepan over high heat, cook mushrooms in hot fat until tender and golden, stirring occasionally.
15. Stir enough milk into meat juice to measure 1 cup; stir in flour, chicken bouillon, and 1/2 teaspoon salt until blended.
16. Add 1/2 cup water to the vegetables in the skillet; stir to loosen brown bits from the bottom of the pan.
17. Pour water and brown bits into the mushrooms in the saucepan.
18. Add meat-juice mixture; cook over high heat, stirring constantly until the mixture boils and thickens slightly; boil 1 minute.
19. To serve, arrange vegetables on a platter with Cornish hens. Garnish with parsley sprigs.
20. Serve with mushroom gravy.
